![][image1]

# **Balloons**

# Concept

The balloons tool allows you to register balloon models that can be handed out in your booth. They are a unique type of item that can be stacked with any other balloon item to form a balloon bundle. A combination of both balloons tied together in the player's hand. They're a great way to spread your brand throughout the map in the hands of players. Balloons can be handed out in 2 main ways:   
A) A balloon vendor, a type of interaction entity you can place that gives the player a balloon on right click with a cooldown. It could be a post with balloons tied to it or an NPC. Whatever you decide, this offers the easiest access. See the interactions API section for more info.  
B) As a reward from interacting with booth content. You can call the balloon give function (summit.balloon:give/\<namespace\>/\<balloon\_id\>) yourself as part of an integrated system in your booth like a store, a minigame reward or anything else. We suggest keeping it fairly accessible as the balloon item type serves the primary purpose of easy to get collectables rather than rare rewards. See the booth definition section for more info.

Balloons should represent your booth in some way, it could be a mascot, your logo or other recognizable elements.

There is no exact limit on the amount of balloons one booth can register but we ask you to keep it light and consider 1 or 2 per booth. If you want more, please ask a coordinator and explain your use case. 

# Style and sizing

![][image2]  
Balloons should follow a fairly strict shape guideline so that players can carry multiple without too much clipping. The larger the balloon, the longer the string needs to be. And we ask of you not to make the balloon any bigger than 1.5x1.5x1.5 blocks maximum. In the attached picture you can see some examples of rope lengths per size:   
8x8x8 pixel balloon=24 pixel (1.5 blocks) rope  
12x12x12 pixel balloon=32 pixel (2 blocks) rope  
18x18x18 pixel balloon=40 pixel (2.5 blocks) rope  
20x20x20 pixel balloon=48 pixel(3 blocks) rope  
These are guidelines, you're welcome to make balloons in whatever shape you want, as long as you keep the size comparable to the listed size categories.

We ask you to style your balloons in a fairly standard way, with a white string and transparent balloon. Ideally the balloons fit together and look somewhat realistic. But creativity is of course welcome\! Do have a look at some pre-made balloons and copy palettes or shapes.

If you don't want to make your own balloon model, we have provided a template model you can simply draw a texture for. See the reference assets.

# Technical model details

When you've made your balloon model, please place it originating from 0 0 0 in blockbench. (This is so that the item definition that combines and rotates the balloons can stack and rotate them properly) North is forward.   
![][image3]  
Then please scale the model down with the scale tool to be 25% the normal size. This will be rescaled again in game, but we do this so the model can fit within the java item model size bounds.   
![][image4]  
And then lastly, before saving, please set all the faces of your model to accept tint index. This allows the balloon to be unshaded and gives it that very see-through plastic look that we're going for. To do this you can download the tint preview plugin on blockbench or set the "tintindex": 0 on each face in the .json model.  
![][image5]![][image6]![][image7]

# Submitting and testing

Balloons are registered, like stickers, via your booth’s booth definition. For specific information of the format to do this, see the booth definition section.
